title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger for publisher confirms, windows, failure ambiguity, and republish. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.
Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence.
Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Confirm Channel Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 5835 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.
